mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding real economic output, which lifts expectations for future corporate earnings and aggregate demand, pushing 10-year Treasury yields upward over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 20-day forward yield change has a negative correlation with INDPRO changes over any 2-year window, or the rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.5%, the labor market is tight enough to generate wage pressure and inflation expectations, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE is below 4.5, the subsequent 20-day yield change is negative more than 60% of the time, or the average change is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is elevated above 245 and the federal funds rate is below 2%, the real interest rate is deeply negative, which encourages investors to demand higher nominal yields to compensate for expected inflation over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the forward yield change is negative in more than half the occurrences, or the average yield change is not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a high level of housing starts (HOUST) signals that the economy is overheating, prompting the Federal Reserve to accelerate rate hikes, which pushes 10-year yields down over the longer 60-trading-day horizon due to front-loaded tightening expectations.
failure_condition: During tightening cycles, instances with HOUST above 1200 do not produce significantly negative 60-day yield changes, or the signal is positive in more than half of such cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) reflect robust consumer spending, which boosts GDP growth expectations and leads to higher 10-year Treasury yields over the next 20 trading days as investors anticipate stronger economic momentum.
failure_condition: The correlation between RSAFS changes and subsequent 20-day yield changes is not positive over any 3-year rolling window, or the rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP (GDPC1) exceeds 17,000 billion chained dollars, the economy is operating above potential, which raises inflation expectations and forces the 10-year Treasury yield upward over the next 60 trading days as the market prices in tighter monetary policy.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 > 17000, the average 60-day yield change is negative or not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicate synchronized strength in labor and manufacturing, which amplifies growth expectations and pushes 10-year Treasury yields higher over the next 20 trading days more strongly than either signal alone.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the average yield change is not positive, or the joint signal does not outperform the average of the individual signals in a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment (FEDFUNDS below 2%), a rise in the CPI (CPIAUCSL) signals that inflation is becoming entrenched, which forces long-term yields upward as the market demands higher term premiums over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 2%, the correlation between CPIAUCSL changes and subsequent 20-day yield changes is not positive over any 2-year window, or the signal is negative in more than 50% of instances.
